title: rubus × hamifer
intro: Rubus × hydridus, more commonly known as the hybrid raspberry or Rubus × hamifer, is a cross between the European raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis), resulting in a unique and versatile plant species. The Rubus × hamifer plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties are believed to contribute to various health benefits, including reduced risk of heart disease, improved cognitive function, and enhanced immune system function. Rubus × hamifer is often prepared as a tea, jam, or syrup, and its leaves and fruits can also be used fresh or dried in herbal preparations.
